summary: Joel Spolsky announces the European leg of the FogBugz World Tour, listing demo events across five cities in November 2007. He also mentions a keynote speech at the Øredev developer conference in Malmö, Sweden, clarifying it will not be a product demo. The post closes with a technical aside asking for help hosting a large Flash video file from a previous tour stop, noting his unfamiliarity with streaming and content delivery networks.
key_points: FogBugz World Tour expanding to Europe with stops in Dublin, Cambridge, London, Amsterdam, and Copenhagen
Joel distinguishes between product demos and his Øredev keynote 'Developing Great Software'
Joel candidly admits inexperience with video hosting and streaming technology, asking readers for CDN recommendations
money_quotes: The morning of November 14th, I'll be speaking at Øredev, a conference for software developers in Malmö, Sweden… this keynote speech is called "Developing Great Software" and is not a FogBugz demo.
I'm kind of new to this video thing.
I think this is the point of content delivery networks.
key_insight: This is a brief promotional announcement for the FogBugz European tour dates with a candid aside revealing that even established software company founders can be newcomers to adjacent technical problems like video streaming.
